title: Callerya pilipes
taxonomy: Callerya pilipes is a species of flowering plant in the legume family, Fabaceae. It is classified within the subfamily Faboideae and the genus Callerya. The plant is native to Asia, specifically in China, Japan, and other regions. It is often found in mountainous areas and is known for its unique flowers and seed pods. As a member of the Fabaceae family, Callerya pilipes is a part of the larger group of plants commonly known as legumes.

intro: Callerya pilipes, also known as the "kakame" or "sarsaparilla vine," is a type of flowering plant in the legume family (Fabaceae) native to Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antidiabetic properties, as well as its ability to aid in detoxification and improve cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Callerya pilipes include saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Callerya pilipes is commonly prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections.
